clinical_coherence.notes: 36M Hispanic with optic neuritis and prior limb numbness. Clinical findings are internally consistent (RAPD, reduced visual acuity, hyperreflexia, Babinski). However, a 36-year-old MALE is listed as taking a combined oral contraceptive (ethinylestradiol/norgestimate) for 'contraception,' which is a significant clinical implausibility. This is clearly a copy-paste error from a female patient template.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI, CSF, VEP, OCT findings are all concordant with MS. Periventricular Dawson fingers, oligoclonal bands, prolonged VEP latency, and RNFL thinning all align with the diagnosis.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G35 correct. Differential, critical actions, and contraindicated actions are clinically sound. McDonald criteria application is accurate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic MS presentation appropriate for straightforward difficulty. Male sex adds slight epidemiologic interest but the case itself is not more complex.

demographic_plausibility.notes: A 36-year-old male patient listed as taking combined oral contraceptives is a major demographic error. The age and ethnicity are otherwise plausible for MS (though slightly older than typical onset). MS is less common in males but not rare.

educational_value.notes: Reasonable teaching case for male MS presentation, but the OCP error undermines credibility. The case structure is very similar to S01.

issues_found: CRITICAL: Male patient (age 36) listed as taking combined oral contraceptive (ethinylestradiol/norgestimate) - clear sex-medication mismatch from template error
Very similar case structure to MS-RR-S01 (optic neuritis + prior sensory episode)
strengths: Tool outputs are internally consistent and medically accurate
Provides male MS perspective in the case set
